heading: Director of Science and Technology
label: 3030 Director of Science and Technology
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 403–3e of this title

Amendments2022—Subsec. (b). inserted at end “In making such appointment, the Director of National Intelligence may give preference to an individual with experience outside of the United States Government.”Pub. L. 117–263

Emerging Technology Adoption, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–263, div. F, title LXVII, § 6703(a)136 Stat. 3562Dec. 23, 2022“The Director of Science and Technology may—“(1)conduct reviews of the policies, standards, and procedures of the intelligence community that relate to emerging technologies and, as appropriate, recommend to the Director of National Intelligence changes to such policies, standards, and procedures, to accelerate and increase the adoption of emerging technologies by the intelligence community;“(2)make recommendations, in coordination with the heads of the elements of the intelligence community, to the Director of National Intelligence with respect to the budgets of such elements, to accelerate and increase the adoption of emerging technologies by such elements; and“(3)coordinate with the Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering on initiatives, policies, and programs carried out jointly between the intelligence community and the Department of Defense to accelerate and increase the adoption of emerging technologies.”[For definition of “intelligence community” as used in , set out above, see , set out as a note under .]section 6703(a) of Pub. children.heading: Director of Science and Technology
children.label: a Director of Science and Technology
children.text: There is a Director of Science and Technology within the Office of the Director of National Intelligence who shall be appointed by the Director of National Intelligence.

children.heading: Requirement relating to appointment
children.label: b Requirement relating to appointment
children.text: An individual appointed as Director of Science and Technology shall have a professional background and experience appropriate for the duties of the Director of Science and Technology. In making such appointment, the Director of National Intelligence may give preference to an individual with experience outside of the United States Government.
act as the chief representative of the Director of National Intelligence for science and technology;
chair the Director of National Intelligence Science and Technology Committee under subsection (d);
assist the Director in formulating a long-term strategy for scientific advances in the field of intelligence;
assist the Director on the science and technology elements of the budget of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence; and
perform other such duties as may be prescribed by the Director of National Intelligence or specified by law.

children.heading: Director of National Intelligence Science and Technology Committee
children.label: d Director of National Intelligence Science and Technology Committee

children.text: There is within the Office of the Director of Science and Technology a Director of National Intelligence Science and Technology Committee.
The Committee shall be composed of the principal science officers of the National Intelligence Program.

children.label: A
children.text: coordinate advances in research and development related to intelligence; and

children.label: B
children.text: perform such other functions as the Director of Science and Technology shall prescribe.
